S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Use Cowork to assemble research lead packets, meeting summaries, decision logs, and briefing documents from scattered so
Distinguish organizing, summarizing, synthesizing, and deciding and know which ones require human confirmation.
· Set up Cowork task briefs that preserve uncertainty rather than papering over it.
· Verify citations, action items, decisions, and sensitive information before using or sharing any artifact.
· Recognize when meeting notes or research materials are too sensitive to feed into a personal Cowork session.

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — CORE CONCEPT: COWORK AS ORGANI

data.title: CORE CONCEPT: COWORK AS ORGANIZER, NOT OWNER
blocks: Organizing grouping, sorting, formatting existing material without changing its meaning. Safest Cowork operation.
Summarizing condensing material, which requires selection. Cowork may drop nuance, uncertainty, or minority views.
Synthesizing proposing patterns or themes across material. Requires the most human judgment to verify.
Deciding turning discussion into commitments, discussion into agreement, options into choices. This is the human's job. Cowork mu

data.title: WORKED WALKTHROUGH: THREE ARTIFACT TYPES
blocks: · A candidate source table (title, author, year, brief description, relevance note)
· A list of search terms and gaps
· Open questions to guide the next phase
· Open every candidate source. Confirm it exists and says what the table claims.
· Fill in the Verified? column only after checking.

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE.
blocks: Citations Does each source exist? Does it say what the artifact claims?
Decisions Were they agreed or discussed? Did anyone object?
Action items Did the named person accept the task? Is the deadline real?
Sensitive information Should any names, details, or quotes be removed before sharing?
Uncertainty Is the artifact claiming more confidence than the source material warrants?

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Treating a candidate source as a verified source.
Letting a discussion item become a decision.
Inferring action item owners.
Sharing without removing sensitive information.
Treating Cowork-generated themes as findings.
